-- THE GPE STREAMING ROUTER UPGRADE (T20 FIX)
-- ==========================================
-- 1. The Structural Upgrade: Add the qualitative 'tier' column to The Warehouse
-- By default, all 13,000+ assets are safely tagged as 'Standard' until you promote them.
ALTER TABLE k_kuts_master 
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS tier TEXT DEFAULT 'Standard';
-- 2. The T20 Mass Promotion (Building the 2-Hour Block)
-- This physically tags your premium K-KUTs so the Streaming Router can find them.
-- INSTRUCTION: Swap these placeholder IDs with the exact K-KUTs you consider premium. 
-- You need roughly 40 tracks to guarantee a 2-hour zero-repeat stream.
UPDATE k_kuts_master
SET tier = 'T20'
WHERE id IN (
    'K-KUT-001',
    'K-KUT-002',
    'K-KUT-SCHERER-001', -- Example: Pulling a Scherer instrumental into the T20 block
    'K-KUT-004',
    'K-KUT-005'
    -- Drop the rest of your premium track IDs right here
);
-- 3. Verification Protocol: Check the exact count of your new T20 inventory
SELECT count(*) AS t20_ready_inventory 
FROM k_kuts_master 
WHERE tier = 'T20';
Source

policies_gpe_ledger

Write
-- GPE ledger policies
DO $$
BEGIN
  IF NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM pg_policies WHERE schemaname='ops' AND tablename='gpe_financial_ledger' AND policyname='gpe_owner_admin_read'
  ) THEN
    CREATE POLICY gpe_owner_admin_read
    ON ops.gpe_financial_ledger
    FOR SELECT
    TO authenticated
    USING (EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.user_profiles up WHERE up.user_id = auth.uid() AND up.app_role IN ('owner','admin')));
  END IF;

  IF NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM pg_policies WHERE schemaname='ops' AND tablename='gpe_financial_ledger' AND policyname='gpe_owner_admin_write'
  ) THEN
    CREATE POLICY gpe_owner_admin_write
    ON ops.gpe_financial_ledger
    FOR INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE
    TO authenticated
    USING (EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.user_profiles up WHERE up.user_id = auth.uid() AND up.app_role IN ('owner','admin')))
    WITH CHECK (EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.user_profiles up WHERE up.user_id = auth.uid() AND up.app_role IN ('owner','admin')));
  END IF;
END $$;

Ledgers, RLS, and Allocation logic have been provisioned

All objects created and RLS enforced successfully. I split multi-action policies into separate operations to satisfy Postgres syntax.

What’s live now

  • Schemas and extensions

    • ops schema created
    • pgcrypto enabled (for gen_random_uuid)

  • Core tables

    • public.user_profiles (role directory: owner, admin, viewer, blocked, receive_only)
    • ops.gpe_financial_ledger (gateway-normalized, net_profit computed, currency, metadata)
    • ops.lb_personal_ledger (beneficiary entries, references (gateway, transaction_id))

  • Indexes and constraints

    • Unique (gateway, transaction_id) on gpe ledger
    • Referential integrity from LB ledger to GPE ledger
    • Helpful indexes for lookups

  • RLS

    • Enabled on user_profiles, gpe_financial_ledger, lb_personal_ledger
    • Access allowed only to owner/admin (service role still bypasses RLS for webhooks)
    • Block-listing is achieved by setting user_profiles.app_role = 'blocked' (no “allow” policies match)

  • Allocation utilities

    • ops.v_lb_allocations produces 1.25% allocations for Mikie and Mattie from net_profit
    • ops.post_lb_allocations(gateway, transaction_id) inserts LB ledger rows atomically
    • Anonymous role revoked on both ledgers

1) Next.js Stripe webhook route (app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts)

Assumptions:

  • You are using Next.js App Router.
  • Environment variables are set in your Next.js project:
    • STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
    • SUPABASE_URL = https://.supabase.co
    • SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY

What it does:

  • Verifies Stripe signature
  • Extracts amounts/fees/tax, standardizes currency
  • Upserts into ops.gpe_financial_ledger with gateway='stripe'
  • Calls SELECT ops.post_lb_allocations('stripe', transaction_id)

Instructions:

  • Create file: app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts
  • Install Stripe SDK in Next.js project: npm i stripe@12
  • Ensure service role key is only used server-side

Code:

import Stripe from 'stripe'
import { NextRequest } from 'next/server'

const stripe = new Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET ? 'sk_dummy' : 'sk_dummy', {
  apiVersion: '2023-10-16'
})

// Always read raw body for signature verification
export const config = { api: { bodyParser: false } } as any

async function getRawBody(req: Request): Promise<Buffer> {
  const arrBuffer = await req.arrayBuffer()
  return Buffer.from(arrBuffer)
}

// Minimal Postgres write via Supabase SQL endpoint using service role
async function execSql(sql: string, params: any[] = []) {
  const url = `${process.env.SUPABASE_URL}/rest/v1/rpc/exec_sql`
  // You can instead call a dedicated Edge Function or a serverless function.
  // For simplicity, we'll use the PostgREST RPC pattern with a SQL wrapper function below.
  throw new Error('Replace with Edge Function call or dedicated RPC. See notes below.')
}

export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
  try {
    const sig = req.headers.get('stripe-signature') || ''
    const rawBody = await getRawBody(req as unknown as Request)
    const secret = process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET!
    const evt = Stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, secret)

    // Normalize event types that represent successful payments
    // Prefer charge.succeeded or checkout.session.completed with payment_status=paid
    let transactionId: string | null = null
    let gross = 0
    let fees = 0
    let tax = 0
    let currency = 'USD'
    let occurredAt = new Date().toISOString()

    switch (evt.type) {
      case 'charge.succeeded': {
        const charge = evt.data.object as Stripe.Charge
        transactionId = charge.id
        gross = (charge.amount ?? 0) / 100
        fees = ((charge.balance_transaction as any)?.fee ?? 0) / 100
        tax = ((charge.balance_transaction as any)?.tax ?? 0) / 100 // often 0; may need invoice for tax
        currency = (charge.currency || 'usd').toUpperCase()
        occurredAt = new Date((charge.created || Math.floor(Date.now()/1000)) * 1000).toISOString()
        break
      }
      case 'checkout.session.completed': {
        const session = evt.data.object as Stripe.Checkout.Session
        transactionId = session.id
        // Gross, fees, and tax may require fetching the related invoice or payment_intent
        // For brevity, use amount_total (gross) and set fees/tax to 0 unless expanded
        if (session.amount_total != null) {
          gross = session.amount_total / 100
        }
        currency = (session.currency || 'usd').toUpperCase()
        occurredAt = new Date().toISOString()
        break
      }
      case 'invoice.paid': {
        const invoice = evt.data.object as Stripe.Invoice
        transactionId = invoice.id
        gross = (invoice.amount_paid ?? 0) / 100
        tax = (invoice.tax ?? 0) / 100
        currency = (invoice.currency || 'usd').toUpperCase()
        occurredAt = new Date((invoice.status_transitions?.paid_at || Math.floor(Date.now()/1000)) * 1000).toISOString()
        break
      }
      default:
        return new Response('ignored', { status: 200 })
    }

    if (!transactionId) return new Response('no transaction id', { status: 400 })

    // Write to Supabase using a secure server-side call.
    // Recommended: call a Supabase Edge Function that runs with service role.
    // Payload example:
    const payload = {
      gateway: 'stripe',
      transaction_id: transactionId,
      gross_revenue: gross,
      gateway_fees: fees,
      tax_withheld: tax,
      currency,
      occurred_at: occurredAt,
    }

    // Example direct fetch to Supabase Edge Function:
    const efUrl = `${process.env.SUPABASE_URL}/functions/v1/ingest-ledger`
    const resp = await fetch(efUrl, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {
        'Authorization': `Bearer ${process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY}`,
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      },
      body: JSON.stringify(payload),
    })

    if (!resp.ok) {
      const text = await resp.text()
      console.error('Ingest failed', text)
      return new Response('ingest failed', { status: 500 })
    }

    return new Response('ok', { status: 200 })
  } catch (e: any) {
    console.error(e)
    return new Response('invalid', { status: 400 })
  }
}

Notes:

  • Best practice is to route writes through a Supabase Edge Function running with the service role, not directly from Next.js. I can deploy an ingest-ledger function that:
    • Upserts into ops.gpe_financial_ledger
    • Executes SELECT ops.post_lb_allocations('stripe', $1)

If you want me to deploy the Edge Function, say “deploy ingest-ledger”.

    • event_type text not null
    • title text not null
    • body text
    • related_entities jsonb default {}
    • occurred_at timestamptz not null default now()
    • created_by uuid references auth.users(id)
  • Indexes:
    • occurred_at
    • event_type
  • RLS:
    • owner/admin can read/write
    • viewer can read
    • blocked has no access

    • Upserts ops.gpe_financial_ledger
    • Executes SELECT ops.post_lb_allocations(gateway, transaction_id)
    • Runs with SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY and bypasses RLS safely
